have h₁ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
    have h₂ : (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) = (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := rfl
    rw [h₂]
    -- Use the fact that the product of the functions is independent given Z
    have h₃ : (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) = fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω) := rfl
    rw [h₃]
    -- Apply the property of conditional independence to the product function
    have h₄ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
      -- Use the given conditional independence to prove the product
      have h₅ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] Y := h
      have h₆ : Measurable Y := hY
      have h₇ : Measurable Z := hZ
      -- Use the fact that the product of the functions is independent given Z
      have h₈ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
        -- Use the property of conditional independence to the product function
        refine' h₅.prod_right _ _
        · -- Prove that the function (Y ω, Z ω) is measurable
          exact h₆.prod_mk h₇
        · -- Prove that Z is measurable
          exact h₇
      exact h₈
    exact h₄
  exact h₁

                -- Use the product property to combine the two

                <;> simp_all [h₆]
                <;> try norm_num
              _ = X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := by rfl

      _ = X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := by rfl
  exact h₃

have h₁ : (fun ω : α => (Y ω, Z ω)) = fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω) := rfl

have h₁ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
    have h₂ : (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) = fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω) := rfl
    have h₃ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
      -- Use the fact that the product space is standard Borel and the measures are well-behaved
      have h₄ : Measurable (fun ω : α ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := hY.prod_mk hZ
      have h₅ : MeasurableSpace.comap (fun ω : α ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) inferInstance ≤ MeasurableSpace.comap Y inferInstance ⊔ MeasurableSpace.comap Z inferInstance := by
        -- The product subspace is less than or equal to the union of the individual subspaces
        refine' le_trans (comap_le_comap _) (le_sup_right)
        intro s hs
        simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe, Set.mem_prod]
        <;>
        (try aesop) <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe, Set.mem_prod]
            <;>
            (try aesop)
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            exact ⟨fun a ha => by aesop, fun a b h => by aesop⟩
          })
      -- Use the properties of conditional independence to prove the result
      have h₆ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
        refine' h.of_mono_right _
        -- Prove that the product subspace is less than or equal to the sum of the individual subspaces
        exact fun s hs => le_of_eq (by simp_all [SetLike.mem_coe])
      exact h₆
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h₁ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
    have h₂ : (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) = (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) := rfl
    rw [show (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) = (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) by rfl]
    have h₃ : (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) = (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) := rfl
    have h₄ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
      -- Use the given condition to prove the new condition
      have h₅ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω => (Y ω, (Z ω : δ))) := by
        -- Use the fact that the product of measurable functions is measurable
        have h₆ : IndepFun X (fun ω => (Y ω, (Z ω : δ))) (μ.toKernel) μ := by
          -- Use the fact that the product of independent functions is independent
          have h₇ : IndepFun X (fun ω => (Y ω, (Z ω : δ))) (μ.toKernel) μ := by
            -- Use the fact that the product of independent functions is independent
            have h₈ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] Y := h
            -- Use the fact that the product of independent functions is independent
            have h₉ : IndepFun X Y (μ.toKernel) μ := by
              -- Convert the condition to an independence condition
              have h₁₀ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] Y := h
              -- Use the fact that the product of independent functions is independent
              exact h₁₀.to_indepFun
            -- Use the fact that the product of independent functions is independent
            have h₁₀ : IndepFun X (fun ω => (Y ω, (Z ω : δ))) (μ.toKernel) μ := by
              -- Use the fact that the product of independent functions is independent
              have h₁₁ : IndepFun X (fun ω => (Y ω, (Z ω : δ))) (μ.toKernel) μ := by
                -- Use the fact that the product of independent functions is independent
                refine' IndepFun.prod_right _ _
                <;> simp_all [Measurable.prod_mk, hY, hZ]
                <;> try norm_num
                <;> try aesop
              exact h₁₁
            exact h₁₀
          exact h₇
        -- Convert the independence to a conditional independence
        have h₁₁ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω => (Y ω, (Z ω : δ))) := by
          -- Use the fact that the product of independent functions is independent
          have h₁₂ : IndepFun X (fun ω => (Y ω, (Z ω : δ))) (μ.toKernel) μ := h₆
          -- Convert the independence to a conditional independence
          exact h₁₂.to_condIndepFun
        exact h₁₁
      -- Simplify the goal to match the given condition
      have h₅' : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
        simpa [Function.funext_iff] using h₅
      exact h₅'
    exact h₄
  exact h₁

have h₁ : CondIndepFun (MeasurableSpace.comap Z _) _ X (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) μ := by
    have h₂ : (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) = (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := rfl
    have h₃ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ Y ω) := h
    have h₄ : (fun ω ↦ Y ω) = (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)).fst := by
      ext ω
      simp [Prod.fst]
      <;> aesop
    have h₅ : (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)).fst = (fun ω ↦ Y ω) := by
      ext ω
      simp [Prod.fst]
      <;> aesop
    -- Use the fact that (Y ω, Z ω) is (Y ω, Z ω) and the projection is Y ω
    have h₆ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ Y ω) := h₃
    -- Now, we need to show that X is conditionally independent of (Y ω, Z ω)
    -- Since (Y ω, Z ω) is (Y ω, Z ω), we can use the projection to reduce the problem to Y ω
    -- and use the fact that X is conditionally independent of Y ω.
    -- This step requires some careful manipulation of the definitions.
    -- For the sake of brevity, we assume this can be done directly from h₆.
    have h₇ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
      -- Use the fact that the projection of (Y ω, Z ω) onto the first component is Y ω
      -- and the conditional independence of X and Y ω.
      -- This is a placeholder for the actual proof, which would involve more detailed
      -- manipulation of the definitions.
      have h₈ : (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) = (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := rfl
      -- Use the fact that the projection of (Y ω, Z ω) onto the first component is Y ω
      -- and the conditional independence of X and Y ω.
      -- This is a placeholder for the actual proof, which would involve more detailed
      -- manipulation of the definitions.
      have h₉ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the projection of (Y ω, Z ω) onto the first component is Y ω
        -- and the conditional independence of X and Y ω.
        -- This is a placeholder for the actual proof, which would involve more detailed
        -- manipulation of the definitions.
        have h₁₀ : (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) = (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := rfl
        -- Use the fact that the projection of (Y ω, Z ω) onto the first component is Y ω
        -- and the conditional independence of X and Y ω.
        -- This is a placeholder for the actual proof, which would involve more detailed
        -- manipulation of the definitions.
        simpa [h₄, h₅] using h₃
      exact h₉
    exact h₇
  exact h₁

have h₁ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
    -- Use the definition of conditional independence
    rw [condIndepFun_iff_inj]
    -- We need to show that X is independent of (Y, Z) given Z
    -- This follows from the fact that X is independent of Y given Z, and Z is trivially dependent on Z
    -- We can use the properties of product spaces and conditional independence to deduce this
    simpa [hX, hY, hZ, condIndepFun_iff_inj] using
      h.prod_right _ (by infer_instance) (by infer_instance)
  exact h₁

have h1 : (fun ω : α => (Y ω, Z ω)) = fun ω : α => (Y ω, Z ω) := rfl
  have h2 : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω : α => (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
    -- Use the fact that if X is independent of Y given Z, then X is also independent of (Y, Z) given Z.
    -- This is because (Y, Z) contains no new information about X beyond what Y already provides.
    have h3 : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω : α => (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
      -- Use the property of conditional independence to extend the independence to the product space.
      have h4 : (fun ω : α => (Y ω, Z ω)) = (fun ω : α => (Y ω, Z ω)) := rfl
      -- Use the fact that Y is measurable and Z is measurable to show that (Y, Z) is measurable.
      have h5 : Measurable (fun ω : α => (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
        apply Measurable.prod_mk hY hZ
      -- Use the fact that X is independent of Y given Z to show that X is independent of (Y, Z) given Z.
      -- This is because the sigma algebra generated by (Y, Z) is the same as the sigma algebra generated by Y.
      have h6 : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω : α => (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
        -- Use the fact that if X is independent of Y given Z, then X is also independent of (Y, Z) given Z.
        -- This is because (Y, Z) contains no new information about X beyond what Y already provides.
        refine' h.prod_right _ _ _
        -- Show that the sigma algebra generated by (Y, Z) is the same as the sigma algebra generated by Y.
        <;>
          (try simp_all [ConditionallyIndependent]) <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try
            {
              -- Use the fact that if X is independent of Y given Z, then X is also independent of (Y, Z) given Z.
              -- This is because (Y, Z) contains no new information about X beyond what Y already provides.
              simp_all [CondIndepFun, inr, Range]
              <;>
                (try
                  {
                    -- Use the fact that if X is independent of Y given Z, then X is also independent of (Y, Z) given Z.
                    -- This is because (Y, Z) contains no new information about X beyond what Y already provides.
                    exact by
                      aesop
                  }) <;>
                (try
                  {
                    -- Use the fact that if X is independent of Y given Z, then X is also independent of (Y, Z) given Z.
                    -- This is because (Y, Z) contains no new information about X beyond what Y already provides.
                    simp_all [ConditionallyIndependent]
                    <;>
                      (try
                        {
                          -- Use the fact that if X is independent of Y given Z, then X is also independent of (Y, Z) given Z.
                          -- This is because (Y, Z) contains no new information about X beyond what Y already provides.
                          exact by
                            aesop
                        })
                  })
            })
        <;>
          (try
            {
              -- Use the fact that if X is independent of Y given Z, then X is also independent of (Y, Z) given Z.
              -- This is because (Y, Z) contains no new information about X beyond what Y already provides.
              aesop
            })
      -- Use the fact that X is independent of (Y, Z) given Z to conclude the proof.
      exact h6
    -- Use the fact that X is independent of (Y, Z) given Z to conclude the proof.
    exact h3
  -- Use the fact that X is independent of (Y, Z) given Z to conclude the proof.
  exact h2

have h₁ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] Y := h
  have h₂ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω => Z ω) := by
    have h₃ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω => Z ω) := by
      apply condIndep_refl_self
    exact h₃
  have h₃ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
    apply CondIndepFun.prod
    <;> simp_all [condIndep_symm, h₁, h₂]
    <;> try assumption
    <;> try infer_instance
    <;> try nonempty_of_mem (by exact ⊤.nonempty)
  exact h₃

have h₁ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω ↦ (Y ω, (Z ω))) := by
    apply h.prod_right
    <;> (try tauto) <;> (try simp_all)
    <;> (try assume MeasurableSpace γ)
    <;> (try assume MeasurableSpace δ)
    <;> (try exact hY)
    <;> (try exact hZ)
  simpa using h₁

have h₁ : (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) = (fun ω => (Y ω, (Z ω))).comp (fun ω => ω) := rfl
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] Y := h
  have h₃ : X ⟂ᵢ[Z, hZ; μ] (fun ω => (Y ω, Z ω)) := by
    have h₄ : (fun ω : α => (Y ω, Z ω)) = (fun ω : α => (Y ω, Z ω)) := rfl
    rw [show (fun ω => (Y ω, (Z ω)).comp (fun ω => ω)) = (fun ω =>(Y ω, Z ω)) by simp]
    -- Use the fact that the function is independent of X given Z
    -- and that Z is already in the σ-algebra of Z.
    apply CondIndepFun.prod_right' h₂
    <;> simp [hZ, hX, hY]
    <;> try infer_instance
    <;> try exact inferInstance
    <;> try exact inferInstance
  exact h₃
